title: Revision Reminder App with Spaced Repetition & Study Growth Tracking

requirements.text: Create a backend data model and actor in Motoko that stores topics, their spaced repetition reminder schedules (1-day, 3-day, 30-day, 3-month intervals from creation time), and a study progress log (timestamp + count of topics revised). Expose the following API: addTopic(name: Text), getTopics(), getDueReminders(), markReminderDone(topicName: Text, interval: Text), getStudyProgress().
acceptanceCriteria: addTopic stores a topic with reminder timestamps for 1-day, 3-day, 30-day, and 3-month from the time of creation.
getDueReminders returns all reminders whose scheduled time has passed and have not yet been marked done.
markReminderDone marks the specified interval for the given topic as completed and records the revision event in the study progress log.
getStudyProgress returns a list of (timestamp, count) entries representing each revision session.

requirements.text: Build a frontend page that allows users to add new topics via a text input and submit button. Display the full list of added topics.
acceptanceCriteria: User can type a topic name and click 'Add Topic' to submit.
The list of all added topics is displayed on the page.
Input field is cleared after a topic is successfully added.

requirements.text: Build a frontend section that displays all currently due reminders (topics whose scheduled revision interval has been reached). Each due reminder shows the topic name and interval label (e.g., '1-day', '3-day', '30-day', '3-month'). Users can click a 'Mark as Revised' button on each due reminder to complete it.
acceptanceCriteria: Due reminders are fetched and displayed when the page loads and can be refreshed.
Each reminder card shows the topic name and interval label.
Clicking 'Mark as Revised' calls markReminderDone and removes the reminder from the due list.
If no reminders are due, a friendly empty-state message is shown.

requirements.text: Build a frontend study growth chart that visualizes cumulative topics revised over time. The X-axis represents time (revision session timestamps) and the Y-axis represents the cumulative total number of topics revised. Use a line chart with data points from getStudyProgress.
acceptanceCriteria: A line chart is displayed showing cumulative topics revised over time.
Chart data is sourced from the getStudyProgress backend call.
Chart updates after a reminder is marked as revised.
If no progress exists yet, a placeholder message is shown instead of an empty chart.

requirements.text: Apply a clean, focused study-app visual theme: use a warm parchment/cream background with deep forest green and amber accent colors, a readable serif or semi-serif heading font paired with a clean sans-serif body font, and a calm card-based layout with subtle shadows. Apply this theme consistently across all pages and components.
acceptanceCriteria: All pages use the defined color palette (cream/parchment background, forest green and amber accents).
Headings use a distinct serif or semi-serif font; body text uses a clean sans-serif.
Content is organized in card-based sections with consistent spacing and subtle shadows.
Theme is applied consistently — no mismatched colors or fonts across sections.

constraints: All backend logic must live in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo.
Do not use any external database; persist all data in stable Motoko variables.
Do not use real-time WebSockets; polling or manual refresh is acceptable for due reminders.
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Mobile app or native app version.
Automatic background polling daemon (user-triggered refresh is sufficient).

required: A minimalist book-and-growth-chart illustration combining an open book with a small upward-trending line chart overlaid, warm cream and forest green tones (study-hero.dim_800x400.png)
